questions.question: Which export formats are available for your entire workspace?

options.explanation: PNG is not an export format mentioned for workspaces. The article specifies HTML, Markdown, CSV, and PDF.

options.text: HTML, Markdown, CSV, and PDF (for Business/Enterprise)
options.explanation: HTML, Markdown, and CSV are available for all plans. PDF export is specifically available for Business and Enterprise plans.
Word and Excel are not mentioned as export formats. The article lists HTML, Markdown, and CSV for databases.
JSON and XML are not export formats mentioned in this article.

questions.question: Where can you find the 'Export all workspace content' button?

options.explanation: The correct path is Sidebar > Settings > General.
According to the steps provided, you go to Settings in your sidebar, select General, and then find 'Export all workspace content'.

options.text: Top right menu of any page
options.explanation: While individual pages have an export option, the 'Export all workspace content' button is located in the Settings menu.
Archive is not the location for exporting content. The correct location is Settings > General.

questions.question: What is the default retention period for pages in the Trash?

options.explanation: Pages remain in Trash for 30 days by default before being automatically removed.
The default retention period is 30 days.
The article states that pages will remain in Trash for 30 days before they are automatically removed.
The default retention period is 30 days; 90 days is not mentioned.

questions.question: Why might the 'Export all workspace content' function fail for a particularly large workspace?

options.explanation: The browser compatibility is not mentioned as the reason for failure. The article cites system limitations for large workspaces.
While unstable connections can cause issues, the article specifically mentions that the function may have trouble completing for particularly large workspaces.

options.text: System limitations for particularly large workspaces
options.explanation: The article states that for particularly large workspaces, the function may have some trouble completing the export.
Admin status is not the reason for the failure. The failure is attributed to the size of the workspace.

questions.question: How can you restore a page from the Trash?

options.explanation: The star icon is used for favoriting pages, not restoring them.
The edit icon allows you to modify the page, not restore it to its original location.
The article instructs you to click the curved arrow icon next to the page in Trash to restore it.
Clicking the delete icon would permanently delete the page, not restore it.

questions.question: What is included in the export to help you navigate the exported workspace?

options.explanation: A PDF guide is not mentioned. The article specifies that a sitemap (index.html) is included.
The article states that 'A sitemap (index.html) is included the export to help you navigate the exported workspace'.
A JSON file is not included. The sitemap is an HTML file.
A CSV index is not mentioned. The navigation aid provided is an HTML sitemap.

questions.question: When migrating content to a new account, what is a specific limitation regarding the top-level page?

options.text: You must delete the top-level page first
options.explanation: You do not need to delete the top-level page; rather, you cannot move it directly.

options.text: You cannot move the top-level page, only sub-pages
options.explanation: The migration steps explicitly state, 'you can only move sub-pages, you will not be able to move the top-level page'.

options.text: The top-level page must be shared with full access first
options.explanation: While you share the page, the limitation is that you cannot move the page itself; you can only move its sub-pages.

options.text: The top-level page will be duplicated instead of moved
options.explanation: The limitation is about the *action* of moving, not the duplication. Sub-pages are moved, while the top-level page remains in the source.

questions.question: How long is the download link valid after requesting a workspace export?

options.explanation: The link is valid for 7 days, not 24 hours.
The article states that the email link 'will expire after 7 days'.
The link expires after 7 days.
While Trash retention is 30 days, the download link for the export expires after 7 days.

questions.question: What is the current limitation regarding automatic backups in Notion?

options.text: Automatic backups are not currently available
options.explanation: The article explicitly states, 'Not at the moment', regarding automatic backups.

options.text: Automatic backups are only available on the Enterprise plan
options.explanation: The article mentions that automatic backups are not available at all, regardless of plan.

options.text: Automatic backups are handled by a third-party integration only
options.explanation: The article states that automatic backups are not available yet, though API updates may enable them in the future.

options.text: Automatic backups require you to install an app
options.explanation: There is no native automatic backup feature mentioned, nor is it tied to a specific app installation.

questions.question: What happens if the exporter doesn't have access to a private page belonging to another user?

options.text: It is included in the export as a placeholder
options.explanation: The article states these pages are 'not included in the export'.

options.text: It is included in the export
options.explanation: Pages without access are excluded from the export.

options.text: It is not included in the export
options.explanation: The article specifies that 'Pages that the exporter doesn't have access to... will not be included in the export'.

options.text: It is automatically shared with you
options.explanation: The article does not mention automatic sharing; it only mentions that admins on Enterprise can grant access to specific pages.

questions.question: How can you restore a page if you accidentally delete your entire account?

options.explanation: Edit history is for restoring past versions of a page, not for recovering an entirely deleted account.

options.text: Contact Notion support for a database snapshot
options.explanation: The article states that support can restore a snapshot from their database backups if you contact them.
While exports are for safekeeping, the article notes that 'You can’t instantly recreate your workspace by reuploading your exported workspace content'.
An entire deleted account cannot be restored from the Trash, which typically contains pages, not the whole account structure.

questions.question: Can you empty your Trash all at once?

options.text: Yes, by clicking a bulk delete button
options.explanation: The article states, 'There isn’t a way to empty your trash all at once'.

options.text: Yes, if you are an Organization Owner
options.explanation: While owners can customize retention settings, there is no button to bulk-empty the trash.

options.text: No, you must delete individual pages
options.explanation: The article confirms there is no way to empty trash all at once; you must delete pages individually or wait for the 30-day auto-delete.
Trash is not emptied immediately; it remains for 30 days (or a customized period) before being permanently deleted.

questions.question: What is the maximum timeframe for which the Notion support team can restore data from their database backups?

options.explanation: The support team can restore snapshots up to 30 days old, which is longer than the 7-day export link validity.
The article specifies that they can restore a snapshot of any page in the past 30 days.
90 days is not mentioned as the timeframe for support team restoration.
The restoration timeframe is limited to 30 days for the support team's database backups.
